title: Prunus urumiensis

taxonomy: Prunus urumiensis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. It is a member of the genus Prunus, which includes trees and shrubs commonly known as plums and cherries. The genus Prunus is further divided into several subgenera, with Prunus urumiensis belonging to the subgenus Prunus. This classification is based on the plant's morphological characteristics, such as its flower structure and fruit type. The classification of Prunus urumiensis is consistent with that of other species within the genus.
uses: Prunus urumiensis is a plant species known to have various medicinal properties. Its bark and leaves are used in traditional medicine to treat several health issues. preparations: Prunus urmiensis, also known as wild apricot, has been traditionally used in herbal preparations to treat various ailments.
